Oh good lord your community post was so tone deaf. “We noticed that you guys aren’t stupid enough to miss the fact that we stealth buffed enemies. We are sorry. It will happen again.”

If you think I’ll not just negatively review this game because you gave me some corporate platitudes… Oh you sweet summer child.

I can’t believe I supported this game. Fix your issues, don’t tell me you will. Do something.

Another wave of corporate PR and damage control is happening due to the reviews spiralling down, again. Deep down no one believes a word of it. You lied to us, paywalled content and vehicles, hid nerfs from us, pretended to buff things while doing the exact opposite through hidden mechanics because of that grunt fantasy you so gladly uphold. You brought this on yourself AH, again. Fix your internal communication issues, get the your teams and managers egos back in check. Time to listen to what the players want.

I loved this game, I still play it regularly with my friends. However...

Since machinery of Oppression dropped, the developers consistently made bad decision after bad decision... I held out hope that things would get better... but the developer AMA was the straw that broke the camels back... 

My biggest issue is the confirmation from arrowhead that they would be focusing on new war bonds instead of fixing core gameplay issues. 

I don't know if these issues stem from Arrowhead or Sony, and frankly, I do not care anymore... I do not recommend spending money on this game in it's current state.

I've seen this pattern enough times now, Countless issues have remained untouched for ages. Arrowhead only seems to prioritize bug fixes or balance when they directly impact new Warbond content. On top of that, we get changes that no one asked for. The state of balancing is so disconnected that it makes  me wonder if they even playtest their own game. It’s frustrating that meaningful communication only ever happens as damage control following a massive backlash. Really love this game, but this time i cant defend it anymore.

The developers at Arrowhead are openly antagonistic toward their own player base, not just through constant shadow nerfs and unnecessary balance changes, but through the way they interact with the community on Reddit and Discord.

Instead of listening to criticism, developers have openly mocked frustrated players, even making comments like “man watching u all cry, amuses me so much.” It’s honestly insane behavior from the people behind a game that made them hundreds of millions of dollars because of the community supporting it.

The constant stealth nerfs have already made the game less fun over time. Every time players find weapons or loadouts they genuinely enjoy, they get hit with undocumented changes or balance patches clearly aimed at making the game feel worse instead of improving weaker options. It feels like the developers care more about policing how people play than actually making the game enjoyable.

I could tolerate bad balancing decisions if the developers treated the community with basic respect, but the attitude coming from Arrowhead staff across social media has been consistently arrogant, dismissive, and hostile toward the people not only keeping the game alive, but making them rich.

Don’t give them another cent until they learn how to treat their player base professionally.

I have done my best keeping the toxicity of the playerbase from swaying me against Helldivers 2 and Arrowhead. All of posts on various platforms complaining about the shortcomings of the game, and yet I still advocated that the game is still enjoyable. I can't keep this mentality anymore, as the issues I have gotten used to and adapted to, have simply piled to high.

What happened to the passion that once was the driving force behind creating this amazing game? Why are bugs allowed to linger for so long? Why is the balance direction keen on nerfing fun? (difficulty, I don't mind if done right, the hardest difficulty shouldn't be a cakewalk) Even now, I find it hard to bash down AH because I understand how difficult it is to maintain a live service game, but having a functional and enjoyable experience is the minimum. please honor your word on listening to the community, as our voices are proof that there is still interest and its all because we love your game.

Initially a great game, especially with a full group of friends, but has since been on a steepening decline. There are several causes: limited testing of new enemies/features, artificially locking central wargame mechanics in stalemate, aggressive moves by publisher Sony, and a fairly dysfunctional relationship between the developers and community.

To summarize each point:

- Updates introducing new gear or enemy types have often released unpolished, causing sudden difficulty spikes in the higher difficulties. New sub-factions often dropped with exaggerated difficulty spikes (From insta-killing fire shotguns to burrowing bugs that cannot be killed until lunging and damaging the player to mechs that demand relentless firepower to destroy while constantly spawning in drops and idle patrols). At the same time, newly introduced gear often launches underperforming next to gear that's been in game for more than two years. Heavy backpack versions of existing weapons launch with little advantage over their counterparts.

-  The game is based around a "galactic war" similar to any strategy game battlemap, but the battlemap is largely controlled by the devs. The way planets are captured or defended by the playerbase heavily relies upon the number of players present. Outside of major orders, you will largely find that the same ten or so planets are always available to fight at, and there is little deviation from those locations.

-  Sony attempted to region lock the playerbase in 2024, requiring all players to use a Play Station Network account to access HD2. Thousands of players in countries unsupported by PSN effectively would have been DRM'ed out of the game. Steam graciously offered refunds to those who would be affected by the region-lock. A tie in with the Killzone franchise also ended up in disaster, as the store items required more currency than would be spent on an entire warbond. After a notably short time in the in-game store, the items disappeared for an entire year and have only recently returned, but now under a "premium" warbond with a higher credit requirement than any standard warbonds.

-  The playerbase is constantly at odds with the developers. Veteran players from the first year have highlighted nerfs to player equipment (, serious performance issues, ridiculous enemy buffs (common grunt enemies receiving heavy armor, enemies that have always had indestructible shields, new enemies with obscure or no weakpoints), abandonment of promised content (No new weapon attachments since first introduction, no customization of armor passive boosts or cosmetics, while helmets grant no bonuses and are cosmetic only, no use for samples after all ship upgrades), fix patches launch alongside new game content warbonds, and warbond system has been bloated with so much content it would take hundreds of hours of farming to unlock it all for free.

This was a fun game that is now teetering on its end of life. New factions pose substantial difficulty curves beyond reasonable difficulty levels, gear is locked behind paid content or countless hours of currency farming, devs and publisher have seriously eroded the playerbase's trust.


If you still want to play, wait for sale.

Arrowhead makes swarm bug units have higher armor ratings than your tanks and mechs. Your stuff gets nerfed while the enemies get buffed. Over and over again. You have to pay a real money to actually get combat effective items but its a 50/50 toss of the coin if arrowhead will nerf that said item in a month or two. Free content is far and few between now and we're constantly ignored by the devs. Fun game, but its being killed off by this studio. Plagued with glitches and unfixed crap. Uninstalled and NOT recommended until the dev's greed and their broken game is fixed. They work on this game like the NPC enemies paid $40-100 for it. Rip off. I would refund all of the super credits I've bought back now if I could.

[h2]Paid for "Super Citizen", Treated Like a Second-Class Citizen[/h2]
I caved to my friends' hype and shelled out the extra cash for the Super Citizen Edition, but right now, I completely regret it. Instead of feeling like a valued player, Arrowhead is treating its core audience like second-class citizens. The game is rapidly going downhill, and it all boils down to a few glaring, unacceptable issues.

[h3]A Bug-Ridden, Anti-Cheat Nightmare[/h3]
First off, the game is absolutely plagued by bugs and hard crashes, and the main culprit is obvious: nProtect GameGuard. Why in the hell does a purely PvE game need a notoriously invasive, kernel-level anti-cheat that acts like malware and constantly conflicts with systems? Mine and my friends' PCs are literally crashing and our entire systems are freezing up just trying to run this. You can spend 35 minutes carrying a mission only to have this garbage anti-cheat freeze your whole rig or crash you straight to the desktop right at extraction. We are essentially paying to be unpaid QA testers while our hardware struggles against unnecessary, system-breaking software.

[h3]Anti-Fun Balancing, Shadow Nerfs & Arrogant Devs[/h3]
The balancing in this game is an absolute joke. It seriously feels like the developers are actively working to make the game unenjoyable. Enemies are ridiculously unbalanced, the mechanics feel unfair, and whenever players find a weapon that is actually fun and viable, the devs immediately nerf it into the ground. 

What's even worse is their habit of rolling out shadow nerfs and then trying to hide them under the pathetic guise of "it's hard to keep track of changes." Jesus Christ, you're a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ triple-A studio! Act like it. Look at their bizarre balancing philosophy: they tweaked enemy damage, effectively nerfing the tank and our mechs so they are made out of wet cardboard now, getting destroyed in milliseconds. One example is the Sterilizer, which isn't exactly a new thing, but it is still utterly useless - it has pitiful damage, zero splash, forces you into point-blank range, and completely wastes a support weapon slot. The community has been begging for ages for it to apply an acid rain effect on armor, but instead, it joins an ever-growing graveyard of useless weapons, stratagems, and gear that have been garbage from the very beginning.

What's more infuriating is how they handle community feedback. I'll give credit where it's due: Pilestedt (the creative director) stepping up to do an AMA takes guts, and I won't bash him for coming forward. However, the AMA itself was incredibly lackluster and completely devoid of any real insight. Reading through their responses, it's painfully obvious that they are stubbornly set on their current path and utterly unwilling to change course based on what the players actually want. 

Arrowhead desperately needs to swallow their pride and take an example from the developers behind Deep Rock Galactic and Warframe. Ghost Ship Games and Digital Extremes actually listen to their players, respect their time, and focus on making their games fun rather than constantly punishing their player base.

[h3]Underwhelming Warbonds, No Transmog & The Super Credit Grind[/h3]
Don't even get me started on the Warbond system and our gear. The cost is too high, the content is severely lacking, and the value for money is abysmal. The gear you get from them feels completely underwhelming and is barely viable on higher difficulties. On top of that, the devs arrogantly refuse to give us a basic transmog system because it apparently ruins their "grunt fantasy" and "visual identity." So we are forced to wear mismatched, ugly armor combinations just to get the passives we need. If you don't want to endlessly open your wallet for ugly gear, get ready for a mind-numbing, tedious grind for Super Credits just to afford a premium pass that isn't even worth it.

[h3]Toxic Positivity, Doxing, and the "Glazedivers"[/h3]
Finally, we need to talk about the absolute state of the community, specifically on the official subreddit. The place is completely overrun by "Glazedivers" - toxically positive fanboys who act like Arrowhead can do no wrong and actively suppress any legitimate criticism.

It has gotten so bad that when one certain player back in March proposed a harmless, fun in-game challenge to the devs, [b][u]he was literally doxed and sent death threats[/u][/b], and the entire situation just spiraled down from there. Instead of taking a hard stance and cleaning house, the moderation and response on the official channels have been a joke, often just resulting in echo chambers that blame the critics while shielding the devs. It is sickening that this kind of unhinged behavior is allowed to fester under the guise of "protecting the game."

[h3]Verdict[/h3]
Unless you enjoy anti-cheat induced crashes, getting your favorite items nerfed, grinding for useless gear, and dealing with a psychotic subreddit of Glazedivers who will threaten your life for criticizing a video game - save your money.

Helldivers 2. For my personal experience of this game, it's been an uphill battle, both in and out of the game itself. Major Orders feel stale, repetitive, and unrewarding. Progression systems like Weapon modifications, Ship Modules, leveling up and even the achievements list have gone stale and feel abandoned. Planets don't feel satisfying to conquer anymore because without 75% of all online players on a single world, it is now virtually impossible thanks to the developers behind the game, and Sony. There's so much missing from this game that the player base has been begging for, Player Hub, Shooting range to test weapons before initiating a purchase, bestiary, and much more that the Devs have a single reply to whenever asked, which boils down to "We may or may not be thinking about it, we can't say much" So much for "being more transparent with the player base". Johan Pilestedt is my favorite person of the team behind Helldivers, but I don't like the fact that he's become this fix-it guy to put in front of the camera for the dev team, he shouldn't NEED to do this. Priorities need to set straight for this game or it will die. As an avid player of this broken, yet beloved game, I do not want this, and encourage the dev team to put all new/ upcoming content aside and focus on the issues players have (players like me would appreciate if the things we already have work as intended than put out more content to potentially break the game again. It's been one step forward, two steps back with this game for a long time, and I'm tired of it). I also encourage the player base be kind in their frustrations, I understand the game has it's charm, but these flaws, like climbing being broken still, progression loops stagnant, and the Second Galactic War becoming stale, are long past due to be dealt with. As for my recommendation to play this game? Not right now, at least until major problems get fixed. I hate to put a bad review on this game, AH, but it's the only way you'll listen.

This game has so much potential, which is why it hurts to see it so thoroughly and consistently trashed by its development studio. 

The devs balance the game like the bug, bots, and squids are the paying customers. It's clear that the writers are tired of people not taking their 'satire' seriously and consistently portray the divers as incompetent expendables that die like hamsters every ten seconds after friendly firing their whole squad, while making balancing changes to reflect this. The trailer for the most recent warbond straight up shows a helldiver in the lumberer barbecuing a SEAF squad for no reason, undermining the massive community effort that went into rescuing the 77th just a couple months prior.

Speaking of warbonds, the way they've been implemented has been super lazy ever since the devs discovered the cash cow that was hiding stratagems behind a price point. Stratagems being unlocked by successful major orders was a great idea that worked perfectly with the community-driven war front mechanic and could have been a powerful driving force to keep the player base afloat, but all the stratagems we've gotten in this way have been completely lackluster. Instead, any actually effective piece of equipment has been consistently locked behind warbonds, which even then often aren't usable upon release or for a time after (like the new armor set) because the devs clearly don't playtest or play the game at all for that matter. 

I will give credit where it is due, and say that the game has come a long way since its rocky launch and there has been a lot of good content added, but the pace at which that content is implemented and the bloat of half usable equipment makes the gameplay loop feel very stale very quickly. 

Helldivers was a huge win for Arrowhead, so the course that the game has taken and the straight up disrespect and disregard they've shown for the community and their opinions has been strange. I hope they get their ♥♥♥♥ together because this game has so much potential, and it hurts seeing it squandered in such a way.

You know it's bad when the loyal Helldivers 2 Youtube channels are branching out to other games for the first time as a backup career plan.

Google "antagonistic dm syndrome" and you have 50% of the reason this game is now hated.

The other 50% is the abandonment of fun in exchange for selling engaging parts of the game back to us that we used to get for "free" and the development team ignoring player feedback out of what feels to be spite and ego. 

Keep in mind no content is really "free" when the game has an entry price of 40 dollars. You are paying for it all with that price tag.

The simple fact of the matter is this: The longer you play this game, the more you will see patterns of laziness from the development team and begin to hate it. Not an ideal flow for a "live service" game asking for consistent visits. This is why people with fewer hours in the game love it and those with more do not.

Right now, we're in the "people complain but play anyway" stage. Next comes the "apathy and a dead game" stage. Here's to hoping they turn the ship wheel sooner rather than later.

Return content to the galactic war, fix the terrible performance issues, stop making older weapons useless through hidden nerfs and above all else abandon the hardcore "grunt fantasy" approach. No one asked for it, no one wants it, but it's all we're being served. 

"We're listening" - Team with no eardrums.
Mech customization should have been a no brainer but AH said that it cant be added to the game then a 16 year old comes a long and proves AH wrong. What the hell are we doing here

I really hate to write this review because i love the gameplay of Helldivers 2 and its concept is fun but oh my god its been in a downward spiral. The game itself i think is fun and is still fun but not as much as it used to be. I think it can be recovered but i don't expect it to happen anytime soon.

First of all the community sucks. Lots of random people are just ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ for no reason, a lot of people gaslight themselves into praising the devs horrid decisions, very few normal debates and conversations tend to happen around Helldivers 2. The devs don't really help because they seem to like adding gasoline to the dumpster fire. The communication in the community and especially between the devs and players is awful. Devs will say they wont do a specific thing and will come up with a workaround to still do what they said they wouldn't without technically doing that thing. For example a gun called the coyote. I personally don't care for the gun but i think the devs pulled ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ around it. Saw people were having FUN with it and said they wont nerf it and then in the very next patch nerfed fire. The coyote, a rifle that does fire damage, was unsurprisingly affected by this change.

The entire idea of balancing in this game is heavily flawed because the most fun things always get nerfed because the devs look at what gets the most usage. Nearly every balance change we usually get affects the players in this pve game, ignoring all of the glaring issues that all the enemy types have and have had for months. Enemies with broken logic and hitboxes? Those clearly don't need to be addressed at all instead have some random weapon nerfs that make no sense.

The devs don't playtest the game at all. Take it with a grain a salt because i might be remembering incorrectly but the devs i think have said they see the playerbase as their playtesters. Yknow instead of the people making the things and actually knowing what needs to be tested because of how many hidden variables are in this game? Speaking of there's so many mechanics in this game that are not explained at all anywhere except for the wiki that the players had to make via DATAMINING TO FIND OUT WHAT THE ♥♥♥♥ DID WHAT.

Lots of the logic in this game also just makes no damn sense. The devs have brought up the concept of realism several times SPECIFICALLY when talking about player equipment. Ignoring the sentient robots and mutant insect population that both basically have their own solar systems under control. The helldivers have to play around "realism" and only the helldivers. And a lot of the logic around the helldivers still isn't even realistic at all. We have a heavy tank that uses medium armor and can be destroyed with a spear. The bots have basically the exact same tank and it requires, who would've ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ guessed, anti tank weapons to destroy. Or heavy armor penetration on its weakpoint.

A recent update that makes me really upset is about the exosuits. I personally don't care about the exosuits. I do care about sentries. A recent patch added a premium warbond centered around exosuits, bringing with it two new exosuits. Since new exosuits were added the devs increased the exosuits health. And for some reason after increasing the exosuit health they also increased the durable damage that enemies do so they can keep up with the health change... Why change the health if you're gonna increase the amount of damage it takes. Oh I know why! ITS BECAUSE SENTRIES DIDN'T GET A HEALTH INCREASE SO THEY WILL GET DESTROYED FASTER BY EVERYTHING BECAUSE THEY WORK OFF OF DURABLE DAMAGE. I DON'T UNDERSTAND THE BALANCING LOGIC FOR THIS ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ GAME.

Another thing adding onto the enemies having obvious issues for a long time that remain unfixed while we get amputated by updates more often. The idea of "known issues". There is a list of known issues that the devs have been updating and working on. These are about bugs in the game. And no not the terminid kind. There are multiple things that everyone who plays helldivers 2 is aware of and is not on the know issues list and should be. A specific one is hellpod steering. When you die and are reinforced you can steer where your hellpod goes to try and get an advantage point if you land in a fight. Except any kind of high ground you cant land on and you will automatically steer away from high ground. This is actually intended by the devs so you cant get up to high ground and not be overwhelmed at close range. But oh guess what even if you use other tools like a jump pack or hover pack to get high ground ENEMIES PHASE THROUGH THE WALLS AND GROUND TO GET UP TO YOU ANYWAYS. This game seems more and more intended to not be fun the longer its around. You play as an "elite force" that have to work like grunts with cool weapons.

One more thing about bugs any playtesting. This game has had issues for a long time about certain bugs applying to you specifically if you are hosting the lobby. This makes no sense how do you even cause that why do you wait multiple months to address it and try to fix it only to introduce three more host exclusive bugs. This game is held together by scotch tape and dry spaghetti.

Oh yeah the premium warbond system and superstore! This FORTY DOLLAR GAME HAS MICROTRANSACTIONS FOR MORE THAN HALF OF THE EQUIPMENT IN THE ENTIRE GAME!!! Technically yes you can find super credits in missions but its so unrealistic to be able to get everything that costs super credits in a reasonable amount of time without buying super credits and even if you did the devs will add another warbond and set of gear in the superstore every month. Each page of the superstore is as expensive as a premium warbond if not more expensive. The game is already 40$ I do not believe that they need micro transactions in their paid game to keep funding it. Its 40$.

Im sorry but if you are considering purchasing Helldivers 2 please reconsider for probably another year. This game is amazing at its core but is handled horrifically and needs a lot of fixing. I personally think the best chance to get it if you don't have it will end up being never or when the devs drop it from live service and work on another game because then the anticheat will be removed and modders can fix them game themselves. I don't believe in happy endings because experience has taught me not to.

I guess nows the time. Spent a lot of time in this game, and played pretty much every up and down this studio has done to this game. It can be fun with friends, and there is a ton of content to explore. Now here's why I wouldn't ever recommend it.

Once you get to the point where you pretty much don't ever die to the enemies actually being difficult(IE your just bad at the game cause your new to it), you'll instead die because you
-Get stuck on an invisible body
-Get rag dolled so often and randomly about
-get stuck under the map and drown
-cant vault away from danger so you just die
and these are just the main ones. 2 of these are bugs that have been in the game since LAUNCH, the other is an actual developer decision to make the player feel like a loser, because nothing is more fun than getting bent over, and railed by a random seed, and the last is a consequence of the developers changing code they don't understand.

Speaking of developers, they are the main reason you should pass on this game. I'm a game developer myself, and knowing how these game mechanics work make it even more clear to me that Arrowhead either consistently lies to their player base, or has no idea how to actually do maintenance on it. 
The Lies:
 - the mechs cant possibly be customizable, nor can you have more than one cause it will implode the game... Modder have been doing exactly this, and proving its viability.
- The whole ordeal about the coyote, and the fire damage change.
These are the main ones

Lets talk about the atrocious balancing, and time it take to get anything fixed, if it ever gets fixed. New weapons, and damage values seem to get changed across the board at seemingly random times. Recently however it been quite obvious that the only buffs to helldrivers equipment take place when the next warbond features it. Its incredibly frustrating to know how fast they can make changes to these values (Because their just integers or floats and its literally as easy has hitting backspace and typing a new number), but they just don't? There have been new weapons released in the primary pool that have the exact same stats as another weapon. Half the weapons arent even viable as is. The CEO talks about weapons being bas on purpose. This is the stupidest thing I've ever heard, from both a player perspective, and a developer. The player gets a load of garbage that isnt fun or engaging and the developers spent thousands of dollars in development time and energy to produce it? Its like there's no QA at the studio at all. This is a reoccurring theme throughout all updates. Not a single warbond THAT YOU HAVE TO PAY FOR BTW has release without half the game breaking or the warbond itself only partially working. Systems that should have nothing to do with the warbond randomly break across the game. The current major annoyance is the breaking of vaulting. Its been months since that broke, and still no one at arrowhead seems to know what's going on. Its like whoever built the game prior to launch has left the studio, and the other interns are trying to read a bunch of spaghetti code and don't seem to know why things affect other things when they try adding something. The netcode especially rarely sees an update if ever. If your hosting the game, you will have a different experience then if your a client. Biggest example of this is the turn radius for chargers, but there are so many more. To me this screams incompetence as a studio, and as an engineering team. The standards must be so low at arrowhead. I would be embarrassed to work there.

I have friends currently new to the game, that struggle to keep up because all the overpowered weapons are locked behind a pay wall, and then another arbitrary resource(medals). You have to spend 40 dollars on the game, then another 20 to 30 dollars just to have the tools to play it??? insane. Even ignoring all that you will now need to navigate all the game changing bugs and glitches that present themselves AND keep up with the games ever changing meta. Havent played in a month? chances are your favorite tools don't work the same way.
And dont even start with the whole super credits are free to grind. The only way to get a reasonable amount was to spends hrs playing a walking simulator on trivial. Even that has been quietly nerfed into the ground btw. Devs were losing money to vets grinding so they took it away. Dont spend 40 dollars on this disaster of a live service game.

Thanks for attending my ted talk.

I'm recommending this game with a huge asterisk, this is not a $40 game.

I really like this game, but the "free" content in this game is a joke. I already paid $40 to get the game, and almost immediately I'm gonna have to pay another $10 minimum just to get the cool stuff.

If you dont have friends to play this game with then the grind for medals to get to the cool stuff is ridiculously slow, solo diving is basically pointless. God forbid you enter quickplay and either die a lot because you're using new/fun equipment, or dont know how to complete the objective, then your team will just not reinforce until they absolutely have to.

BUT, if the stars align and you have $60 and some friends to play this with, I think it is one of the most fun games out right now.

I have played this game since launch. I have seen every single mistake and misstep that this developer has made. I have seen the highs and the lows and at this point I am exhausted. The way the devs "balance" this game is maddening. One step forward, one step back and nothing of value changes. It's hard to remember at this point but Arrowhead was once seen as "one of the good ones" in terms of monetization. Now it's a free for all, selling individual mechs and charging a premium, premium exclusive weapons, and much more. When was the last time something became avalible for all players? There have been bugs, glitches, and unfinished features in the game SINCE DAY ONE. Arrowhead's blind devotion to making this game a grinding and unpleasant experience, and their bizarre belief that there needs to be faction balance in a PvE game is genuinely infuriating. They piss out new warbonds in a futile attempt to distract us from the massive issues that they are responsible for. 

All is not lost, however. With well placed buffs across the board, QoL updates, and certain enemy nerfs, this game could go back to being great. Time will tell if that happens, and based on how Arrowhead has reacted in the past, I have my doubts.
Poor NPC gonna quit the game if players have strong weapons 😔

Developers have been shooting themselves in the foot for the past year or two. Optimization is bad and only got slightly better a few months ago. The community is full of larpers who defend billion dollar companies like they're getting paid and will dox people for having opinions other than their own.

Unfortunately it will never get better since half the playerbase will refuse to hold the developers accountable for anything and the other half will keep coming back like abused housewives.

I would only ever pay a maximum of $20 for this game in it's current state. Only way to enjoy it is either as friendslop or if you're larping.

The game came out as something amazing. Really. I loved it.
Then it all started.
"We don't want players to run META builds all the time", they said.
Little did we know Arrowhead would force a META themselves.

After they did a big buff patch, the game was really fun again! The illuminate introduction was amazing. The Stratagems being bound to MO was also a really cool thing to fight for! 

But, of course I wouldn't give a negative if the game was all sunshine, democracy and gunfire. The game was in a pretty good state for a good time. We defended Super Earth, but then things started to crumble imo. To get the good stuff you gotta spend money, or farm for a long time. A really long time. And even though they said "everything should be viable to some extent", going higher in difficulty narrows down your effective equipment so much, everyone runs AT with chaff clear, SMG? Useless! LMG? Why take that if you can get HMG or Stalwart, it does nothing better. Shovel as a warbond weapon? Blegh! Sniper? Nigh useless except against bots (who somehow are the most "fun" to go against atm). So many bugs, enemies phasing through walls, alerting without even seeing/hearing you. Full armor vehicles dying by the touch of an insect, but a Warstrider has no real weakpoints, needs 2-3 C4 to blow up, can't be fought without super AT. Even the VOX had weakspots so you can fight it without support weapons. The Hulk and Charger are so well designed. Clear weakspot, heavy armor, big damage, but fun to fight! Not like Ragdoll machine 9000 VOX and Warstrider....

The game got repetitive, and boring. 
And with it, came not buffs, not interesting MO like DSS (though that was an insane letdown aswell and is only useful to get full ammo without taking a booster, boosters are btw really bad and theres like 7 good ones, the rest is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥)
With the game stagnating came.....what exactly? Nerfs? A really uninspired EXO warbond thats just a a mech with different arms? Why not make them modular and unlockable with an MO instead? Hackers showed us whats possible?

[h1] I wanted to play and enjoy this game when it dropped. Now I don't know anymore. [/h1]

Back when this game came out, Helldivers 2 went beyond Arrowhead's expectations when the game was so damn popular that the servers couldn't even handle so many people trying to play at the same time. It was an unexpected success, with how it's premise of fighting for democracy while actually being a totalitarian state underneath and how unseriously it took itself. While I couldn't be there to fight at Malevelon Creek (Xbox player at the time, later moved to pc), the fact the community banded together to liberate a single planet, doing so in the process and becoming such a massive meme of being Space Vietnam that the devs even added a cape to commemorate those who fought in the planet's liberation was absolutely amazing. It was amazing that no major order was needed to send the Helldivers to one specific planet, all who went, chose to do so. And that was during a time when some bot units were far more annoying to deal with (gunships) or didn't exist yet (war striders). 

[h2] The bumps begin. [/h2]

Issues started happening between the community and Arrowhead when the forced linking of PSN accounts and removal of access in well over 150+ (iirc) countries had occurred, and as a result the game got review bombed to force Sony to end the forced account link and bring the game back to the countries it was banned from suddenly. This is where my knowledge of drama from here up to the hive world Oshuane major order is spotty, I didn't keep up with the drama until recently, if something happened then that you didn't like and was unmentioned, this is why. While the community was able to make the link not required to play, I don't remember if the game was made available again in the countries it was suddenly removed from. 

[h2] Monkey's paw balancing, illogical/unlisted changes. [/h2]

You probably expected a section like this, so let's just get into it. While I wasn't there to experience it myself, there was a method of dealing with chargers that involved using an AT weapon to blow off the armor of a chargers legs and shooting the now squishy flesh to kill chargers. A method that used intended game mechanics to deal with a particular enemy, no glitches, no exploits, just a rocket, some lead and you have a dead charger. And in the 1.001.100 update, an unlisted change was made to chargers. Their exposed leg flesh received an increased armor value from 0 to 2 (light), and the durability of this part went from 30% to 70%. Why? Why would you make an unexpected way of dealing with a particular enemy that didn't exploit unintended mechanics harder to do? Why would you punish players for finding new ways to deal with enemies? The obvious weakpoints of a charger are its head and its butt, typically dealt with by using AT on these parts, and while Arrowhead intended players to target these areas, they didn't expect players to find different ways to deal with the enemies they made, and instead of congratulating the players and encouraging them to find new ways to take down their foes, they punished player creativity instead. A change I don't like is the heavy devastator's shield now being destructible. It should have been destructible since day 1. It's a shield, hit it with something hard enough and it will break, it can't be that durable to eat several AT rounds and still function as if nothing happened. Another change players didn't like was the loss of control while steering their Hellpods when being reinforced and being spawned much further away from the beacon. This change just doesn't make sense unless you play city missions a lot, and even then, it's still not good. This change seems to have been made to prevent players from getting on top of high cliffs inaccessible even with a jump pack, seemingly to keep players from taking advantage of their environment to call in sentries on the high ground, use as cover or distract heavies so teammates can exploit weakpoints. This essentially made the upgrade for greater Hellpod steering useless. Again, why? Why take options away from players? And the changes made to fire around the Coyote discussion are just sad. When the Dust Devils warbond dropped, people picked up the Coyote and found it to be a reliable weapon, as not only was it an assault rifle with medium armor pen (3), it also fired incendiary rounds, setting the enemies of Super Earth ablaze. Around this time, people were worried Arrowhead was going to nerf the Coyote, as they had a track record of nerfing things and buffing enemies, and begged them not to, and Arrowhead made that promise. While waiting, some members joked that the Coyote was going to be nerfed. Come the next update, and that's what happened, sort of. While the Coyote never got nerfed directly, changes were made to how fire damage worked, including higher enemy resistance to being set alight, so that was a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ lie. In the sake of balance, Arrowhead made the Helldivers weaker while making the enemies stronger. 

[h2] Realism [/h2]

For some odd reason, Arrowhead wants to make the game more realistic when it works against the players, but ignores realism when it comes to enemies. One of these 'realistic' effects is the effect holding the One True Flag, making enemies target the flagbearer more over other Helldivers. Changes have been submitted to Arrowhead about giving the flag a morale boost effect, making the already fanatical Helldivers fight even harder against the enemies of democracy. Yet Arrowhead shoots down these ideas, claiming them to be unrealistic. It's not as unrealistic as they think. Real life armies have been inspired by their flags throughout history and have been used as a rally for troops, why wouldn't this make Helldivers fight even harder? It doesn't even need to be something like a damage boost or increased armor pen, just something like increased reload speed, damage reduction, or less stamina consumption. And a personal experience on stealth that I just ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hate, enemies immediately knowing where you are when they didn't see you when you destroy their ♥♥♥♥. One time, I was doing a bio-processer side objective, I move around, placing c4 on fabs, processers, and a hulk, I move to an elevated position decently far, crouch, detonate, and the bots immediately know where I am. HOW? WHY? If Arrowhead wants realism in their game, then it should affect enemies too, if they don't see me and their ♥♥♥♥ blows up, they should look around to find who caused their ♥♥♥♥ to blow up. I know this isn't Metal Gear or Splinter Cell, but if there's going to be an option for stealth, then there needs to be some better stealth mechanics. And do I really need to mention the charity doxxing fiasco? What the hell Arrowhead? Why didn't you gut the Reddit and Discord mods when this happened, to send the message that this behavior will not be tolerated? Just so disappointing, and other reviews cover areas I missed, and I just can't play a game that punishes creativity and playstyle over the intended gameplay loop, and devs that don't take action against despicable community behavior. ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hell Arrowhead, do better. The game will not last on this course.

Title:
Helldivers 2 in 2026: Lightning in a Bottle… That Arrowhead Keeps Dropping
Review (Not Recommended):
I loved this game when it launched. The chaotic co-op, the over-the-top stratagems, the “managed democracy” satire — it was pure magic. Two years later it’s May 2026 and I’m writing this with a heavy heart: Helldivers 2 feels like it’s actively fighting its own players.
Here’s the reality:
• Bugs everywhere. Invisible enemies (especially on Gloom maps and Automaton landmines that are back again), enemies clipping through terrain, desynced hitboxes on Chargers and Fleshmobs, soft-locks in caves and mega-cities, stratagems bouncing like rubber balls, invisible walls, and random crashes. Some missions are literally uncompletable because objectives break. Xbox players are eating constant CTDs.
• Performance is still terrible. Frame drops, audio bugs, particle spam crashes, and the game “breaking at the seams” after every big update. Arrowhead even delayed content to “focus on stability”… and we’re still here.
• Balance is incoherent. Weapons flip between god-tier and useless patch after patch. Enemies get buffed while player tools get nerfed. Every update feels like it undoes the last one instead of actually building the game. Melee kills still don’t count properly in stats.
• Progression is dead. Max level feels empty. Samples are basically useless once the Super Destroyer is upgraded. No real endgame, no meaningful rewards for high-difficulty play. Super Credits are a pain to farm on anything above medium.
• Warbonds over everything. New paid cosmetics and weapons keep dropping while core issues (weapon customization still unfinished since 2025, missing Super Destroyer upgrades, unused Bureau Terminals, repetitive Major Orders) sit ignored for months. It genuinely feels like the studio cares more about selling the next battle pass than fixing the game we already paid for.
• Small but infuriating stuff: stratagem input bugs, loadouts carrying over, POI icons not updating, host/client desync making status effects and detection completely inconsistent, anti-cheat drama, etc.
This used to be the best co-op shooter on the market. Now it feels like Arrowhead is snatching defeat from the jaws of victory every single quarter. The core loop is still fun when the game actually works… but that’s happening less and less.
I really, really hope the devs read the review bombing and the community feedback and finally put fixes first. Prioritize stability, give us real progression, finish the damn weapon customization, and stop treating paid Warbonds like the main priority. The potential is still here — but right now Helldivers 2 is in a sad state and I can’t recommend it until Arrowhead shows they actually care about the game we fell in love with.
Super Earth deserves better.

It's getting harder and harder for me to recommend this game due to how arrowhead (AH), the developers of the game, have been treating it and the community with it.

[h2]Balancing[/h2]

The balancing in this game suffers from "monkey paw" problem, where the developers will [i]technically[/i] achieve the results the community wants, but not in a way, that would benefit anyone. 2 clear examples are the coyote AR and the mechs. The community begged AH not to nerf the coyote and AH [i]technically[/i] didn't nerf it, only increased fire resistance for all the enemies, which diminished coyote's stand out feature.
With the recent release of exo experts all the mechs received balance changes, that buffs the mechs in a way, that'd make them viable on higher difficulties, however, all enemies received additional buffs to all transport. The mechs remain practically unchanged and all non-mech transport is now weaker. Thanks Arrowhead!

[h2]Monetization[/h2]

The current monetization of HD2 reminds me of a F2P game with it's monthly $10 warbonds. At the time of writing this review the total sum of warbond near a $200 mark, which is insane for a game with a $40 upfront cost. The only alternative to spending $10 on a super credit pack is to find them in missions, which turns an otherwise fun game into a mindless chore. "Why reward the players for contributing in the war effort when they can play difficulty 2 for the next 15h and get their new toys that way? Because otherwise we get no paycheck straight from the customer's pockets" said my strawman of Arrowhead. 
And, sure, with the amount of mediocre or irrelevant items in warbonds, one would think to only buy the ones necessary for a pleasant experience, but AH continue to lock more feature behind a pay/grind wall, starting with armour passives to stratagems, to possible weapon attachments, to vehicles and time will tell if they dare to put future player progression behind a paywall as well.

[h2]Progression[/h2] 

Up to level 20, the game has constant unlocks, that keep you playing, but after that.. hey, have you heard about warbonds? Requisition, samples and player level have no meaning past level 50 as your ship is fully upgraded, all stratagems are unlocked and weapon attachments lack the utility to be worth grinding and unlocking. That only leaves super credits for warbond acquisition and medals to unlock the rewards in said warbonds. 

Helldivers 2 started as a gem, but as time went on, more and more dirt has been piled on top of this gem, which is truly a sad sight to behold, especially for me, as someone, who had participated in HD2's story from it's release.

Helldivers 2 had everything going for it  chaotic co-op fun, satisfying gunplay, and that signature over-the-top satire. At its core, the game *should* be a blast. And honestly, when it works, it really does shine. But that’s exactly what makes the rest of it so frustrating.

Because the biggest enemy in Helldivers 2 right now isn’t the bugs, the bots, or the battlefield  it’s the developers themselves.

The pattern is exhausting. Promises get made, the community gets hopeful, and then we’re handed something completely different. It feels like a cycle of sweet little lies followed by silence or damage control. Instead of actually listening, it’s like they hear the feedback, nod politely, and then go do the exact opposite anyway.

Balance patches are a mess. Weapons people enjoy? Nerfed. Strategies players have fun with? “Adjusted” into the ground. Meanwhile, actual issues that the community has been pointing out for weeks just sit there untouched. It creates this weird feeling where you’re not fighting enemies anymore — you’re fighting the game design decisions.

And the communication? That’s where things really fall apart. There’s a huge disconnect between what the community is saying and what the developers seem to respond to. Players aren’t asking for miracles  just consistency, transparency, and a sense that their time and feedback actually matter. Instead, it feels like we’re being talked *at*, not *with*.

What makes it worse is that the community genuinely *wants* this game to succeed. People are passionate about it. They’re sharing ideas, testing builds, sticking around through rough patches  and it feels like that effort just gets ignored. That kind of disconnect doesn’t just hurt the game; it slowly burns out the player base.

It’s frustrating because Helldivers 2 isn’t a bad game. The foundation is strong. The gameplay loop can be addictive. The co-op moments can be incredible. But all of that gets overshadowed by decisions that feel out of touch and a lack of trust that keeps growing every update.

Right now, it feels like the developers are their own worst enemy. Until they start actually listening  not just hearing, but *listening*  the game is going to keep stumbling no matter how good its core is.

And that’s the real disappointment here. Not that the game failed, but that it *doesn’t have to*.

I have been playing Helldivers 2 since launch, and I played the original Helldivers on PS4 and PC.  I love these games.  Helldivers 2 is still an overall fun game to play.  However, I can't recommend Helldivers 2 in good conscience to new players because of how Arrowhead Game Studios has been handling it since it released.  I have given them the benefit of the doubt for as long as I could, but actions speak louder than words, and their actions scream that they simply do not care about fixing, maintaining, and making their game better.

TLDR:  Helldivers 2 is full of bugs/crashes and most likely always will be, monetization is the only end-game progression, and Arrowhead Game Studios is awful when it comes to making meaningful changes to the game in response to player feedback.

Helldivers 2 is an unoptimized, buggy mess.  Arrowhead Game Studios does not prioritize optimization, and polish.  The game has a mountain of technical debt which has only grown with each update since the game came out.  Every update brings more bugs and crashes.  Whenever the developers do attempt to fix some issues, they only ever fix a few items at a time, barely putting a dent into the plethora of fixes the game really needs.  These fixes usually come weeks, months, or sometimes even more than a year after the issues initially show up.  What's worse is these fixes typically accompany paid extra content releases relevant to the issues they address.  So unless Arrowhead Game Studios stands to make some extra cash from fixing something, they will not fix it.  They have put off addressing their mile-long list of known issues for so long now that I doubt they will ever make any meaningful progress toward fixing them.

Helldivers 2 is a paid game, but most of the new content is locked behind paywalls within its warbonds, the game's paid extra content packages.  Warbonds typically contain new weapons, emotes, cosmetics, and stratagems at $10 a pop for "premium" warbonds, and $15 for each "legendary" warbond. As of early May 2026 when I am writing this review, this game will run you about $220 (price of game included) if you want to gain access to all of its content without grinding for Super Credits (in-game currency).  This figure does not include how much you would spend on separate items from their Super Store, so in reality you're looking at spending closer to $300 if you want to collect everything.  Super Credits can be grinded out, but the most effective method of doing so is mind-numbingly boring search and collect on the lowest difficulties over and over again for hours on end.  Most players would rather earn their Super Credits from playing on their favorite difficulties, but the earn rate is a slow trickle at best this way.  Neither warbonds nor Super Store items ever go on sale.  Helldivers 2 is a paid live-service game with free-to-play monetization.

This monetization becomes Helldivers 2's main source of end-game progression once players have finished grinding out its free content.  The free content I'm referring to are the free Helldivers Mobilize warbond available upon purchasing the game, unlocking ship modules (upgrades to your Super Destroyer which are basically passive buffs for your stratagems earned through collecting in-game samples), and leveling up your favorite primary weapons to unlock all their attachments.  For competent players who play regularly, this can all be accomplished before they reach level 75 (half the level cap).

The galactic war (the system which players participate in to drive the game story forward) is mostly unrewarding, and often seems rigged, removing any feeling of meaningful contribution to Helldivers 2's narrative.

Arrowhead Game Studios claims to be listening to their players' feedback, and sometimes we do see results in-game, but this is very rare.  The developers often make questionable changes to the game which only serve to undermine player feedback and requests.  Weapon customization was a long-requested progression system, and it has barely been updated since its introduction into the game.  Not to mention most weapons have a very small selection of attachments to grind for.

I could go on, but I think you get the point if you've read this far.  I've fleshed out all the things about this game that concern me the most, and I'm sure there are other reviewers touching on aspects of the game I missed.

Sorry, Arrowhead Game Studios.  I honestly hope you can turn things around, but it's been 2+ years of the same self-destructive patterns over and over.  I was rooting for you, but you've depleted all the faith and good will I had for this game.  Good luck!

Hey guys!!! Okay so, Helldivers 2 is a game with 4 factions. The main 3 are Terminids (the bugs), Automatons (think terminator), and the Illuminate (legit aliens). Let me just say that this game is so cool and provides plenty of unique moments. I think defending Super Earth was one of the most amazing gaming experience overall (for me). 

Now, let's get to the 4th faction we are currently up against (why I cannot recommend this game). The biggest threat to Super Earth is its community and DEV team unfortunately :(

Before I start, these are just my observations (please don't send me death threats for this review lol). I normally play solo with no mic since I don't really have friends (and I am afraid of people). I really don't want this to be a repeat of the horse guy. I'll break this review into 2 parts showing the strengths and weaknesses of both parties involved. Sorry with the parentheses!

1) The Community!

Strengths: For the most part, my interactions with the community have been super good in-game (sometimes there is toxicity, but its not too bad). I think unity has bonded us so many times to face our enemies (whether that was on Angel's Venture or even Meridia before it turned into a purple donut). My favorite experience was when practically the whole squad abandoned a pretty hard mission, leaving me and one other guy to complete it ourselves at 0 revives. Seriously, what a great time!

Weaknesses: We cost horse guy his job and volunteer position (I still feel bad about that sorry for bringing it up), and his love for this game. It will never be okay to do stuff like that (destroying someone's real life). When the hivemind decides to get angry, it sort of swallows up literally everyone in its path. And we have been getting angry for pretty much the last 3 years with this game (its not the first review bomb, but I think its probably going to be my last). There's just too much negativity for this to be an enjoyable experience for literally anyone who plays this game casually. Also, we really don't like the DEV team's choices (to be fair, there's a lot of choices that just don't make sense). I won't really get into those choices too much (the other reviews do a great job, and I don't have much to add there in terms of timeline).

2) The DEVS

Strengths: First of all, the fact that you guys are so resilient is AMAZING!!! It takes a really strong crowd of people to be able to see their vision come to life, especially with all of the hate you guys have to face on the daily. I probably wouldn't be able to handle a fraction of the negativity you guys faced. I actually really liked the cave update (it was super unique) as well as the Hive lords (god, they are so cool). I remember immediately hopping on to try the illuminates when you guys announced it during the game awards (I think), and that was such a crazy experience.

Weaknesses: You guys just don't listen to your community AT ALL. I think you guys might actually hate us tbh. There are no long-term rewards or even short-term rewards. There are no ways to earn exclusive cosmetics, increasing FOMO and gatekeeping within the community. Look, I have all the cosmetics outside of the Malevelon Creek cape (and I actually don't want it). Do your new players a favor, and have them complete challenges to earn these cool rewards for themselves (sorta like DRG)! I have the golden eagle set and I want more people to rock that drip (especially with the Oshuane cape OMG ITS SUCH A VIBE). The player base is already divided/alienated enough right now. Of course, I'm only going to speak on the exclusive stuff after the Malevelon Creek (because again I have them and I feel like that's fair for me to comment on). I fought on Super Earth so that those who fight after me can wear that cape with pride knowing that their fallen fought tooth and nail for their home world. 

Too many damn nerfs and you guys know it. You guys add weapons into the game and pretty much forget about them entirely, leaving us with a meta build that is incredibly boring. I challenge you guys to make all weapons actually fun to use. I still think light penetration in this game is extremely lame (bullets just bounce off if the armor level of the enemy is too high). Especially when our Mechs literally break to those same level penetration weapons (our stuff is far less durable than the enemy's stuff and that is sort of an issue). AND speaking of the mechs, we got those from a major order. You guys actually found a way to make customizable options a paid equivalent (the mechs could have been so cool if we could have unlocked them in a major order). bruh. I'm not even going to start on the flamethrowers (fire in general) or the fact that we can no longer dive into high terrain (our hellpods legitimately are locked in place/steering them does not work). Weapon customizations are extremely limited. You won't let us customize armor perks, so that we can run any armor set for just their looks. You guys pride yourselves on listening only to the low sodium helldivers reddit for game balancing. If you really cared, you would take the time to read through every single review (on literally everywhere) to find out why exactly your game is failing. Your community thinks you don't care :(

I can't handle death threats over a video game opinion (I'm really sorry, I'm just scared of you guys at this point). Have I been unreasonable?

Full Review: Helldivers 2 is a game whose bones are so unbelievably solid its still extremely fun after years of mismanagement. However, as a multiplayer game the experience of playing it is highly dependent on the community and the discussions happening around the game. And therein lies the critical flaw with how Arrowhead has managed the game. HD2 has been painstakingly managed in such a way that they'd end up with a player base always primed for an explosion. As a game developer, one might feel its necessary to make unpopular changes to ones game for balance or in anticipation of other future changes, I accept that completely. However, one might expect that in an experience as openly buggy as HD2 with so many incomplete systems and oodles of tools that are never picked, some highly requested features might be regularly trickled in as well to ensure a consistently improving game experience. One might expect that in a game based on an extremely punishing  loop whose difficulty is primarily derived from how easily a mistake cam kill you, bugs and outright imbalanced aspects which disrupt that gameplay loop and cause your death even when you are doing everything right would have at least a little priority.

However, it does not matter how egregious a bug, how useless a tool, or how needed a change may be, Arrowhead will only consistently deliver when being review bombed. Basically any new issues in the game can be expected to linger for months or years unless the players immediately make their displeasure known, and features the studio has promised only seem to materialize when arrowhead needs to desperately regain player trust. Features that were functional and fun at games launch have simply been broken for months, issues which Arrowhead hasn't even indicated they care to fix despite consistent player feedback (looking at you hellpod steering). Meanwhile, when players come up with new ways to play the game or are too vocal about how much they enjoy certain pieces of equipment, it will be nerfed next patch, and such nerfs will be justified by saying they were player requested even though we can obviously tell that such feedback was the minority, if it existed at all. This is obviously going to create a community primed to explode on regular basis. Idk what they expected when running the game like this- they certainly had plenty of time to observe this cycle forming and nip it in the bud, but now their stuck with a community extremely sensitive to changes they don't like and somewhat sparing with praise. Now that they've dug this grave for themselves, they could put literally ANY effort into managing the player backlash before it starts by including requested buffs with nerfs and throwing in the features they know players want AND HAVE TALKED ABOUT ADDING SINCE RELEASE but they... simply choose not too. As I mentioned at the start of my review, like many multiplayer games this game is highly dependent on the vibes of its community, and Arrowhead seems content with a community brimming with resentment and rage. Which is a bold choice for a multiplayer game you hypothetically want people to keep playing. 

Various interviews and AMA's before and after the games release have made it clear Arrowhead set out to make a niche game, and accidentally made a popular one. I don't begrudge them their creative vision, quite the opposite in fact. Arrowhead, if you want to develop a more brutal grunt fantasy game with less spectacle, you ideally should have released the game in that state, but absent that you should just commit. Accept your gonna loose the vast majority of your players and make your vision. If pressure from Sony or other outside forces are preventing you from doing that by forcing you to change course anytime you loose too many players, as many in the community suggest, you need to accept the consequences of being beholden to people who don't care about your creative vision and make the game the community broadly desires. Right now, you are giving everyone involved the worst possible outcome,  as the mainstream players are treated like cash cows who are regularly lured back when needed, and the players who hypothetically desire the game Arrowhead envisions are constantly having their experience compromised for people who are growing more polarized and resentful of that vision, and proceed to bring that resentment into the game. Its simply not sustainable.

I use the word "sustainable" deliberately. This cycle will not be sustained for much longer. Make no mistake, I genuinely believe that this game has time to regain community trust, break the current extremely toxic dynamic that's formed, and dominate the top sellers list for years to come. I don't think it takes a genius or a massive AAA studio to see the vision of how that happens either- HD2 is so fun that even at its worst it provides more satisfaction than most games ever will, and its ceiling can be so, so, so much higher. But are nearing the point that so much damage will have been done, so much resentment fomented, the game will never recover from the toxicity that's poisoned its core. 

To summarize, purchasing Helldivers 2 at this point in time is the video game equivalent of making a new best friend whose also terminally ill and refuses to get proper treatment because of their "personal convictions". Fulfilling, but also detrimental to your long term mental health. If that sounds like a blast then this is the game for you!

I love this game, the gameplay loop is fun and with friends it's an absolute blast for the first 20-30 hours. That being said I cannot recommend this game. It's clear now that the audience Arrowhead wanted and ended up with are not the same, the devs are antagonistic and outright malicious when it comes to community feedback even as far in the early days of the game mocking player complaints of unnecessary balancing and nerfs to the community with "gitgud" & "I know I'll have to talk to HR for this but its a skill issue."

The Galactic War:
 When the game first came out it had real weight behind it and we were rewarded or punished depending on failures or success with either losing or gaining stratagems or even great backstory and events. Now nobody cares about the war, there's nothing to fight for and the modifier has only exacerbated the issue by making where you had to have tens of thousands of helldivers on a planet to do anything. Whenever we do make strides or do something out of the "choices" of the GMs we are punished in one way or another by it meaning nothing and just basically resetting or the GMs coming our of left field to wipe all progress out unless we get the rest of the player base to help which is impossible with no way to organize groups, clans or battalions.

The Nerfs:
We are nerfed both publicly and behind the scenes at every turn be it directly or by damage and numbers. Whenever the community shows gravitation towards equipment or weapons the devs cant help themselves but to hone in on that items and adjust it directly or behind the scenes. We used to be able to stim instantly saving you from stupid deaths and now there's a delay on it and even when you hear the audio from the injector you will still die. When they add a vehicle like mechs or the tank destroyer they cant help themselves but to buff enemy durable damage which is damage done by the enemy to player vehicles but also extends out to sentries and enemy equipment like shields. Before the enemies would ignore our vehicles unless we got in them which would help with the fact they were made out of paper mâché but now they even start taking fire before they're dropped off and in some cases if the Eagle 2 pilot didn't drop it off immediately it would be destroyed before it even hit the ground. They removed our ability to pilot our pods to land anywhere we wanted, restricting us to a smaller sections and no longer able to land on mountains or buildings and if we happen to do it anyways they made those surfaces unable to hold stratagems. There are so many other instances of nerfs but that's just what I will mention here.

Your Time:
This game absolutely does not respect your time. The weapon progression is bare bones and a joke, some weapons don't even have attachments to them and if you level them up to max you get camos for them. The weapons take longer to level up than battlefield 6 when it first came out and is absolutely unacceptable. Medals are capped at 250 which is how you unlock items in the warbonds which items are ridiculously overpriced. Same thing with the samples and the ship modules, we haven't had an expansion to the ship modules in 2 years so most players have maxed out samples with nothing to do with them outside the DSS.

The Devs:
As is stated in my intro, the player base they got is not what they wanted. They don't listen to feedback and ignore it or outright do the exact opposite just to be antagonistic. Bugs that effect enemies in a negative way to the players are left alone for months, sometimes years where bugs that are beneficial to the players are gone in weeks at most. The only time they actually make changes is when they face backlash and even then its always 3 steps back and one forward. They promise transparency and communication while constantly changing values and numbers behind the scenes and staying silent. The GMs want you to play one way, their way, if we buck their design even by a little we are brutally punished and they actively take revenge on us. They have shifted focus to monetization and are now in the mind set that warbonds are now content, major updates or battles are also content but we have no progression or real reason to stay. Commissar Kai said it best "They force feed us cake when we need soup" They continuously tell us they will do better and try to fix progression but we've heard that for the duration this game has been out, their words mean nothing. They actively hate you and aren't afraid to prove it.

Performance & Balancing:
Performance has always been an issue with this game and it seems like it gets worse with every update. The engine is now discontinued so they are trying to make it work by adding on to the code however it also seems like they have outsourced and contracted out a lot of their work so I'm convinced they don't know how the code works or changes effect it because they didn't write it. They already admitted to not playing the game so they have no idea how the different difficulties work or how many enemies of what type spawn so the only type of balancing they're interested in is balancing us. When a redditor challenged the devs to play a match of every difficulty with a specific loadout to highlight how the loadout items needed to be buffed the dev himself admitted that he didn't realize you had to play the previous difficulty to unlock the next one. Then "Glazers" doxxed him, got him fired from his electrician job and banned form the horse sanctuary he volunteered at and what was the reddit and devs responses? Ban future player requested challenges and remove everything from the challenge and stop it immediately. I keep saying this, they hate you and they want to make sure if you are vocal about your displeasures of the game you will be punished. After the Super Earth event they did an update which made the game unplayable on 40 series cards where it would crash your GPU, this issue has never been fully resolved. I use to run this game no problem and now I crash on certain missions or 1 of every 10 drops. No other game has this issue other than GreyZone Warfare but Madfinger fixed the issue within a week.

In conclusion I cannot recommend this game due the devs and their unwillingness to listen to the community and even make it worse and they want to make one type of game and you can take it or leave it.

I genuinely cannot understand how you keep getting this wrong.

You had lightning in a bottle and decided to pry the lid off yourself. You consistently snatch defeat from the jaws of victory, and at this point it doesn’t feel accidental anymore. The balancing is incoherent. Weapons swing between feeling incredible and being completely unusable, with no consistency or clear design direction. Enemies get buffed while player tools get nerfed, and every update feels like it undoes the last instead of building on it.

There is this constant “Monkey’s Paw” approach to every change. Any time something feels good, it immediately comes with a tradeoff that makes the overall experience worse. Something gets stronger, but the enemies get stronger too. A weapon becomes viable, but something else gets gutted to compensate. It feels like you’re afraid to let the game be fun without pulling it back. Instead of fixing long-standing issues, you keep pushing out Warbonds while core systems remain broken. And those same Warbonds from over two years ago are still full price with no meaningful adjustment. That’s not support, that’s stagnation.

The live war, which is supposed to be the core identity of the game, barely feels alive anymore.

Cyberstan was the breaking point. A major event that lasted only a few days, cut off right as players were getting invested, and ended in a way that made the entire playerbase feel irrelevant. It should have been a long, meaningful campaign that people could rally behind. Instead it felt like a scripted loss with no payoff. Worse, the way reinforcements were calculated actively caused friction in the community. Players were punished for engaging with the game normally, while others were harassed for not participating in a system that didn’t even track progress correctly. That is not a community issue. That is a design failure.

We haven’t had meaningful, engaging events with new equipment in ages. Bugs have remained in the game for years. Not months. Years. At this point it’s not an oversight, it’s a lack of priority. What makes this especially frustrating is that the community has been clear. Not vague, not unreasonable. Clear. Players have explained what works, what doesn’t, and why. You have the data to support it. You can see exactly what people use and what they avoid, and yet nothing meaningful changes.

At some point, you either commit to the game you made or you don’t. Right now, it feels like you don’t. It feels like you’re trying to push the game in a direction no one asked for, while constantly undermining what actually made it successful in the first place. I’ve been playing since the first Helldivers. That game was stable, focused, and knew exactly what it wanted to be. This one feels like it’s constantly fighting itself.

How do you go from a fun, niche game with a clear identity to something bigger, but worse in all the ways that matter?

Because this halfway approach isn’t working.

The game still has a strong foundation. It’s still fun at its core. That’s what makes this so frustrating. You can see how good it should be, and then watch it get dragged down by inconsistent decisions and a lack of direction. Stop overcorrecting every system. Stop undermining your own design. Let the game be what it was clearly meant to be.

For the love of God, please just listen to your player base, and fix your game.
