The Fall of Helldivers 2 needs to be studied. As a day one player who praised the game for going against the curve with its original ideas, its prioritization of player agency and in general making a fun game I can honestly say that is no longer the case. There are so many issues that this game has amassed over its lifetime its become clear to me and many others that this game needs a serious change to ever become like it once was.

1. Balancing 
   The balancing in this game falls victim to the same rhetoric as many other games. Instead of making difficulty an option for those who want it, the developers force it onto every aspect of this game and expect the player to enjoy it. An example of this is the weapons that the game has to offer. The developers have made every weapon and stratagem in the game is some way "mid". Most primary weapons cannot deal with some of the most basic and spammed enemies that the game wants to throw at the player. While I understand that if there was a singular weapons that could handle all things would make the game trivial, there seems to be no leeway on this matter. The few primaries that do have the leeway and can actually hold their own quickly become the best and seem to be the most picked weapons out of the game. With there being so few that fall into this category, the game quickly becomes stale is this department using the same things over and over against the same enemies that the player have seem and thousand times before. Many say that no weapon is bad its the player's skill that makes those weapons good is a cop-out for good game design. I as the player should not look as the vast options of weapons this game has to offer and feel dread at the thought of using a weapon that I know I'm going have to fight against the entire time I have to use it. The developers have fallen into the same trap as so many others by thinking that difficulty is the same as giving players mid to bad equipment and inflating the enemy's health and armor as good game design. Moreover, the developers have seem to have forgot what this game was marketed as and what it should be. They seem to think that the players want a hard game as if its akin to Darksouls when in reality that is not what got the game popular in the first place. Even after receiving backlash after every update they have put out and a ever decreasing number of concurrent and active players somehow the developers have continued to force feed the players with contrivances that is confused as difficulty. The game does not feel difficult it just feels annoying to play.

2. Glitches and Bugs
   Over the course of this games lifetime there are still so many issues that even on day one release the developers have not fixed. Here are some things that I have experienced. There many times where there are invisible walls and obstacles either caused by terrain errors or enemies that have large hitboxes that do not disappear after death and can cause  the player to become stuck in place with the only option to get out is to die and respawn or these can block bullets the player shoots and can potentially make the player kill themselves when using a weapon that is explosive. This is not difficulty nor is it realistic and makes me roll my eyes every time it happens because this has been an issue since day one. Another one is reloading and using stims and picking things up, there have been so many times since day one where I have to press a button multiple times before the game decides to do the action. This is not just a me problem i have seen people complain about this very issue since the games release and it has yet to be fixed. This issue costs me so many deaths that I'm sick of it. I could be standing still doing nothing fighting nothing and i have to press the reload button several times before the game reads it. It happens so often that the games intended way of stopping the player from reloading and stimming such as flinching and ragdolls that its just another layer of annoyance that i have to go through to play this game. Another example of this incompetence is respawning. Ideally and what it used to be is that when someone throws a respawn beacon for you it will drop you down in a hellpod within a set area around the beacon which then the player being respawned can control where they land as they are coming down. However, the devs saw that this was being used to get onto things such as building and tall rocks to try and gain an advantage against the enemy. So the devs wagged their finger and said no no and proceeded to make the game less fun by sometimes taking control away from the player and not allowing them to land where they want. This inadvertently cause issues like not being able to control your hellpod at all or the hellpod being spawned way farther away from the beacon essentially creating another layer of annoyance. However, the devs didn't stopped there they decided to remove the ability to climb up to high places saying not you're not allowed to have fun which then caused another issue that i find so ridiculous. For some reason with the update to not be able to climb onto some things there are moments where i will be ruining and come across a tiny lip of terrain that the game say i cant climb. Even if this lip doesn't lead to a mountain or some really high place its just an ankle high rock that the game has deemed that you cant climb. This has gotten me killed so much and adds so much annoyance to the game. Another thing that the devs decided is that you cant call in stratagems onto elevated so you can be on a tall rock and call in weapons or place sentries onto them. The game sometimes think that even the smallest elevated position is something you cant call in stratagems so the ball will sometimes bounce away from the player making so that you kill yourself by calling in something explosive or just missing a target completely or making it so that the weapon called in is unreachable or in the middle of enemies. Just another way the devs removed fun in order to add another layer of annoyance. These are issues that should've been fixed a long time ago but have not and for some reason the devs have decided not to do anything about these things and other issues that haven't mentioned in favor of adding more crap to the game.

3. New "Content"
   The devs have decided that the best way to keep this game alive is the add a bunch of warbonds that only one to four things are useful or interesting instead of adding to the already existing content. Im just going to list off some things that players have wanted that devs have denied in favor of a new warbond. More customization of primaries, customization of secondaries and grenades, customization of armor colors and passives, customization of mechs and destroyers and ships, new ship modules that have been the same since day one so that sample actually mean something again, something to spend requisition slips on, more levels beyond level 150, new game modes that are factions specific and that combine factions so that the player are fighting two enemy types at once or factions that have captured other factions experimenting on them thus making a new enemy that is a combination of the two, human enemies that have sided with certain faction since there are illegal broadcasts and science that seem to suggest that people of super earth are siding with the enemy, various buffs to certain weapons that no one uses, more difficulty game modes beyond just 10 that spawn more enemies, and more. But the devs seem to want none of this and continue to take away player agency, inflate difficulty,  keep core issues with glitches and bugs whilst adding more warbonds that cost $10 that can sometimes have useless items that will never be buffed that no one will use.

It is almost impressive how Arrowhead managed to kill the golden goose and blow both their legs off in one shot.

They're going to be writing case studies about this game in a few years.

The developers continue to snatch defeat from the jaws of victory.

Used to find this game incredibly fun. At one point it was one of the best horde shooters I've played next to deep rock galactic and left 4 dead. During those days, I found the gunplay realistic yet fun, enemies challenging but well balanced, and every gun viable in it's own unique way. Plus, progression was great once too. Samples had a purpose and a majority of the games stratagems and weapons were free. Even with the first warbonds, they used to feel fair, optional, and not to mention completely free to obtain with enough effort. Back then, I was happy to buy some in game content despite the option to get it freely because I felt like Arrowhead had respect for me and my time as the player. Hell, even the community was very supportive and welcoming then too. Creative solutions could be found for every in game problem by driven divers such as using the railgun on charger limbs for a more effective kill, events like saving the children's hospital and earning the mechs were entirely community driven making the galactic war feel involved, and nothing was considered a broken or kick worthy strategy by the wider player base. Those times were awesome.

Now though, I've fallen very out of love with this game. The balanced weapons disappeared update after update as whatever weapons the community liked such as the rail gun, flame throwers, and coyote got nerfed into uselessness. At the same time, once weak weapons like silencers, flamethrowers, and now mechs receive temporary buffs as soon as they get a dedicated warbond. Weapons that, might I add, that used to be good already before Arrowhead nerfed them. Arrowhead is literally solving problems they caused in balancing over and over again, and the warbond system which once was a beacon for ethical live service monetization has now lost all my respect because of it. Especially now that the meaningful progression which used to be earned through sample collecting in missions has been left untouched for longer than I can remember. The last truly free update that didn't require spending supercredits was the recent addition of the bastion tank, and even then, that's only one free stratagem in a sea of warbonds you have to grind many many many hours for. 

I am a man with little free time, as you can probably tell from my hours in this game despite having been with it for about a year. So I don't care what the glazers tell me, I am not wasting hours of my life monotonously farming for supercredits on difficulty 1 just to afford the new warbond, and then farm more for the medals from major orders to actually recieve the warbond's contents. FYI, I've only earned 4 warbonds including the free one in my entire time playing this game normally without supercredit farming. 80 hours just to finish the free one, then grind for two more that are now not meta viable anymore. there are 23 total warbonds in the game right now. So players like me are locked behind an immense pay/grind wall just to access weapons that make the game fun again. but that's not my only issue.

Remeber the balancing issues? well that's not the only problem with the game. bugs are still everywhere while arrowhead continues pushing more and more warbonds instead of meaningful updates like rebalancing and bug fixes. there are still tons of enemies in the game who can hit you through walls, especially within the illuminate, and ragdolling is still a major fun killer made worse by the inclusion of many new enemies that ragdoll you more often than ever before. That, and every rebalance they do always breaks something else if they do rebalance. It still baffles me that they put heavy armor impenetrable to most weapons on common hiveguard's yet still can't be ♥♥♥♥♥♥ to give the powerful vehicle stratagems any armor above medium (yes, that means the tanks and walkers can all be damaged and destroyed by common ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ infantry, because arrowhead refuses to give them armor that's stronger than paper). All this talk of bugs and balancing is also made worse by the fact that glazers have effectively hijacked the once friendly community, making any discussion and criticism impossible. For crying out loud, someone got ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ doxxed for making a lighthearted challenge to the devs to play their own game, and the devs did mostly nothing other than release a useless statement. So attempts to offer constructive criticism to the devs directly is out of the question now.

Lastly, there's the fact that once community driven events have now faded. Players actions don't contribute much of anything anymore. Much like a controlling dungeonmaster, Arrowhead railroads the playerbase into the results they want. No longer can the communities combined efforts liberate the next important planet like the creek, oshaune, and super earth through determination alone. Now the developers seem to force the results they want like on cyberstan where despite overwhelming participation we lost, and none of our contributions meant anything. the war that once felt controlled by player efforts now feels like a scripted series of events the devs want. Even then, events like stratagem unlocks tied to major orders and minor events like that famous save the children hospital one are now extinct. following the next major order just doesn't feel worth it anymore. The only point now is grinding for the medals for the next warbond.

So overall, I doubt Arrowhead is going to listen to players like me and likely you as a beginner who will see these problems themselves too. The game is getting worse with every update, and while I'm holding out hope that things change, I don't see light at the end of this tunnel. So, I can't in good faith recommend playing Helldivers 2 anymore.

Helldivers 2 is not the game it used to be.

There was a time where the players and the developers used to like each other. After months (around a year now?) the developers have been ignoring player feedback whether it be for balance or fixes, either dodge the answer or lie to us about changes that get made in the game, and have been pushing for monetizable content over real gameplay, and completely leaving over 45% of the player base in the dark for 'endgame content', instead prioritising the monetizable content. 

To start, I'll lay down the craziest fact. There are countless bugs in this game that have existed for years. Some of them date to the launch of the game. And yes, the developers are fixing them, but only for them to get broken again literally 1 month later. A guided missile launcher was unable to lock properly for around 7 months, it got fixed and was working fine, just to stop working again after a few months. Your player character getting rag-dolled was in a frustrating and annoying state for 8 months, got fixed, broken again 1 month later. Game audio has been working inconsistently for the past 8 months. The fix? "Just go into your game files and increase the RAM bro" is the official developer response, and an official fix has not been heard about. On the topic of game performance, the only real optimisation efforts were made earlier this year and parts of late 2025, and has been abandoned since. The game runs over 65% worse than it did on launch. Hardlocking issues, where your pc completely shuts off due to reasons related to the gameguard or actual helldivers game (I'm unsure about the real cause), continue to plague people in the community, with my own friend's pc shutting down twice in 1 game about a week ago, as if running minimum settings 720p with a pc well in the reccomended specs list isn't enough. All of these issues mentioned have not been acknowledged since they were initially fixed.

Not only that, but the developers are increasingly deceptive as time goes by. We have been told on multiple occasions that they are "listening to community feedback" and willing to work with us. Very few changes are made in regards to feedback, with even less effort put into fixing bugs. Why? Because fixing bugs doesn't bring in money for Arrowhead, and Balance isn't done based on community feedback; the CEO of arrowhead has openly stated that they balance based on pickrates, not on what 'influencers' say. Not only is that ignoring the feedback they're asking for, but these so called 'influencers' spend days with their communities on stream building balance and glitch feedback forms to try and perfect what the right moves are to do, but its almost like they just get sent straight into the paper shredder, just like the rest of the forms sent in. Next, the devs dodge the balancing decisions they announce and sometimes straight up lie about changes they make. The most well known example is the changes on a popular gun, the 'Coyote', they openly stated they didn't touch the Coyote. What actually happened? They changed it so its harder to light things on fire with it, which is literally its entire niche. That change also affects a few other guns in the game, but they aren't mentioned at all. 

Next, we get free major updates around every 4-6 months. Thats fine. But what isn't fine is the paid/"grindable" content that releases monthly, which technically is free but requires multiple in game hours on a mind-numbingly boring difficulty to just unlock a single warbond. This used to take around 50-80 minutes, after it got changed it now takes 3-4 hours, if not more. For a company that was previously seen as "fine to support and spend $10 for" it doesn't take a genius to figure out how greedy Arrowhead has become over time. Not only that, but good balance changes and relevant bugs are only fixed when its time to release paid content for. For example; 2 months ago we got a warbond with a backpack flamethrower; in the trailer it looks awful, and the community backlash was significant enough to make arrowhead make it increadibly strong so the warbond would make sales. Around 2 weeks ago, we got a warbond with mechs. Not only should mechs have been added through the game via major orders to give players something to rally and fight for, it was locked behind a paywall/grindwall. The average player will have to pull out their credit card or spend a week grinding a boring difficulty just to try the new fun gear, which in previous years we unlocked through the games major order system, which was both more fun and also brought more reason to play the game. Not only that, but before the mech warbond was introduced, mechs really sucked. They felt like they were paper thin for a description that claimed they were "heavily armoured" (which they infact have medium armour where enemies of similar sizes have heavy armour) and were able to be killed in 1-3 shots with medium sized weaponry. After the warbond, they were changed to be much tankier and feel a lot better, which is now clear to the players that they will only make reasonable and good balance changes when it will help the devs make money. For a game that only uses "good FOMO" (an impossible concept) this is just unacceptable.

To end off with, if we put aside warbonds, there is 0 endgame content. For the average player, you will unlock every free stratagem in the game at around level 25-30. You will unlock every upgrade for your ship at around level 80-90. And your favourite weapons will be fully customised by the time you finish your ship upgrades as well. How many players are above level 90, according to internal statistics? 49.3%. 49.3% of players are being cut off by the devs because making profitable "progression" is a higher priority than keeping the active player counts up and giving players a reason to play the game. For some, progression isn't needed to play the game, but for the majority of casual players, the major order system isn't enough to keep them coming back. On the topic of the storyline, it used to be far more meaningful. We would get a new stratagem to unlock if we liberated a certain planet, we had the choice between unlocking antitank minefields or saving some sick kids (we saved the kids) and we've stopped aliens from destroying our homeplanet by coming through a wormhole that was rapidly approaching. That meant something. Now, the major orders that mean something are rushed through and poorly planned, with the recent cyberstan invasion, the final push for the capital was on a Thursday. A huge portion of players who are only able to play on weekends completely missed one of the most exciting ingame events that had happened for months just because the devs were half rushing the invasion and didn't think it through. The excuse? "Good FOMO". In addition, we got a tank a few days prior to the invasion, but we just 'got it'. We didn't have to go rush to liberate a planet for tank parts, or defend a new factory from an invasion, we just got it. Huge missed opportunity to rally the community and give players a reason to log on for the first new free stratagem in over a year. But because the devs were rushing out the cyberstan invasion event, we were just given it and that was that. Its very clear that because the tank wasn't going to make the devs revenue, it wasn't given enough thought to be released properly, just like the Cyberstan event.

Overall, I love this game, and I wish I could recommend it and tell people to buy it. But with the current direction its going in, I really just can't do that. If you're looking for a squad hoard shooter experience, I hear Far Far West is doing Far Far Better right now. If you have a choice between the two games, I will say please consider that one more than Helldivers 2.
